bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting my maximum-utility bid by holding the top value on every issue. The opponent’s opening offer uses valid values but gives me low utility across all issues, and their message about fairness is not supported by the numbers, so I will counter firmly.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still extremely early at t=0.02 and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeating an offer that is quite poor for me despite calling it balanced. To avoid simply repeating my previous all-best bid, I make a small concession on issueC while holding firm on my highest-value positions elsewhere.

I am targeting a high-utility bid of about 0.842 because it is still very early, and the opponent’s offers remain extremely low-value for me despite their claims of balance. Their latest numerical move did not meaningfully improve my outcome, so I am making only a small concession from my prior offer while holding firm on the most important issueA and other high-value positions.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.84 because it is still very early at t=0.06 and there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s offers remain extremely low for my preferences and their claimed “movement” on issueD actually reduced my utility, so I will trust the numbers rather than the cooperative language. I am making a small variation from my previous offer by conceding on issueC while holding firm on the most valuable positions for issueA, issueB, and issueD.

I am targeting a high-utility early-game bid while making a small reciprocal concession, since time is only 0.08 and there is no need to rush toward the opponent’s very low offers. The opponent has held firm on issueA and issueB at valueF but has moved on issueC and issueD; their “balanced” language is cheap talk, but the bid history suggests issueC/issueD are where movement is happening. I will keep my strongest positions on issueA and issueB while offering movement on the lower-priority tradeoff space.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.73 because it is very early (t=0.10) and the opponent’s offers remain quite unfavorable to me despite their “balanced” language. Their actual bids show only limited movement, mainly on issueC and issueA, so I am holding firm on my highest-value issueA and issueB while making moderate concessions on issueC and issueD and avoiding repeating my last exact bid.

At t=0.12 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid for myself while making a modest concession on lower-priority tradeoff space; this bid gives me about 0.706 utility. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me despite their “balanced” language, with only small movement on issueC/issueD, so I hold firm on my most valuable issueA and issueB while moving issueD further from my ideal.

I am targeting about 0.71 utility, which is still appropriate this early at t=0.14 while showing movement on lower-cost tradeoffs. The opponent’s offers have improved only slightly for me and remain low-value overall, despite their “balanced” language, so I am not conceding on my highest-priority issueA or strong issueB. I am switching issueD to valueF, which costs me no more than my prior issueD concession and matches what the opponent has repeatedly proposed.

At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a strong utility while making a visible concession from my ideal bid. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me, but they have shown repeated interest in low issueA/issueB values and some movement on issueC/issueD, so I concede issueA from valueA to valueB and keep issueD at valueF while holding firm on issueB.

I am targeting a moderate concession while preserving value on the highest-weight issueA, since we are still early at t=0.18 and the opponent’s offers remain low-utility for me despite their “balanced” messaging. Their bids show some movement on issueA, issueC, and issueD but they have held issueB at valueF, so I am conceding on issueD and partially on issueB/issueC without moving all the way to their position.

At t=0.20 it is still early enough to target a high-utility package, around 0.69 for me, rather than matching the opponent’s low-value offers. The opponent’s bids consistently insist on issueB: valueF and have sometimes moved to issueC: valueC, so I am conceding on those while holding firm on my highest-weight issueA and compensating with issueD. Their “balanced/win-win” messages are cheap talk; the actual offers show only limited concession.

I am targeting a moderate early-game utility around 0.65, conceding mainly on lower-priority issueB and partially on issueD while holding firm on my most important issueA and strong issueC. The opponent’s messages claim balance, but their actual offers remain low-utility for me, with only modest movement on issueA/issueC, so at t=0.22 I should not concede too deeply yet. This bid avoids repeating my last exact offer and tests whether concessions on issueB and issueD help move them.

I am targeting a moderate concessionary bid with utility around 0.56 on the weighted scale, keeping my highest-weight issueA at valueA while conceding substantially on issueB and issueD. At t=0.24 there is still no need to accept the opponent’s low-utility offers, but their history shows they strongly prefer issueB: valueF and often issueD: valueF/E, while their “balanced” messages are cheap talk rather than binding evidence.

At t=0.26 there is still no deadline pressure, so I am targeting a moderate-high utility bid rather than dropping to the opponent’s very low-utility packages. The opponent’s actual offers show some movement on issueC and issueA, but they remain quite firm around low values for issueB and issueD; I am therefore holding my most important issueA at valueA while making concessions on issueB/issueD and keeping issueC at a value they have repeatedly signaled.

I targeted a moderate-high utility bid (about 0.58 weighted utility) because we are still only at t=0.28 and the opponent’s offers remain low-value for me despite their “balanced” messaging. Their bids suggest they strongly favor low-end values, especially on issueB and issueD, so I am conceding those while holding firm on issueA, my most important issue, and keeping issueC reasonably favorable.

At t=0.30 I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.51 rather than accepting the opponent’s latest low-value package for me; there is still time, but I should show meaningful movement. The opponent’s offers have slowly improved for me, especially on issueC and sometimes issueA, while they continue to push hard on issueB and issueD, so I concede on those lower-priority areas and keep issueA at a strong but not maximal value.

I am targeting a moderate concession around the low-0.4 utility range, since we are still before the midpoint but the opponent has shown only limited movement toward my preferences. Their actual offers remain weak for me despite repeated “balanced” messaging, but they have signaled possible acceptance around issueA=valueD, issueB=valueF, and issueC=valueD, so I am packaging those while preserving value where possible.

I am targeting a moderate concession around the mid-0.4 utility range for me: better than the opponent’s latest low-value offer, but more accommodating than my earlier high-utility positions. At t=0.34 there is still time, so I should not collapse to their preferred all-F/E pattern, but their recent movement on issueB and occasional movement on issueC suggest a package with issueB=valueD and issueC=valueD may be more plausible. Their messages repeatedly claim “win-win,” but I am relying on the actual bids, which show slow concessions rather than true balance.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.45: enough concession to keep progress at t=0.36, but still preserving value on higher-weight issueA. The opponent’s offers remain low-value to me despite their “win-win” messages, but they have signaled possible flexibility on issueA=valueD, issueB=valueD, issueC=valueC, and issueD=valueF, so I am packaging those without repeating my exact prior bid.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.46: enough concession for mid-game, but still well above the opponent’s current offers to me. The opponent has gradually moved from all valueF toward issueA:valueC/D, issueB:valueD/E, and issueC:valueC/D while holding issueD near valueF, so I am conceding issueD and using values they have already signaled on the other issues rather than relying on their “balanced” messages.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.55, improving over my last bid without jumping back to an uncompromising opening position. At t=0.40 there is still time, so I should not concede too deeply; the opponent’s latest offer finally moved issueB to valueA, while they have previously offered issueA=valueD, issueC=valueD, and issueD=valueF, making this package grounded in their revealed concessions despite their repeated “balanced” messaging.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.57, conceding on lower/less recoverable points while preserving value on issueB and issueC where the opponent has already shown possible flexibility. The opponent’s offers remain low for me overall, but they have signaled willingness on issueA=valueD earlier, issueB=valueA at t=0.40, and issueC=valueB now; their “balanced” messages are cheap talk, so I rely on the bid history instead.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.55, making a real concession from my earlier high-utility bids while still protecting value on issueB and issueC. The opponent’s offers remain low for me and their “balanced” messages overstate the actual numerical movement, but they have signaled possible flexibility on issueA=valueD, issueB=valueA, issueC=valueD, and issueD=valueF across prior bids. This package combines values they have previously offered while avoiding repeating my last bid exactly.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.57, conceding substantially from my ideal but still protecting value on issueB and issueC where the opponent has recently shown willingness to move. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim balance, but the actual offers remain low for me; their latest useful signals are willingness on issueB=valueA and some movement on issueA/issueC, while they strongly prefer issueD=valueF, so I concede issueD.

At t=0.48 I am targeting a mid-game compromise around utility 0.66 for me, conceding on issueD while holding stronger value on issueA and issueB. The opponent’s latest offer shows meaningful movement on issueA and issueC, but still keeps issueB/issueD mostly low for me; their repeated “win-win” messages are cheap talk, so I am relying on the offer pattern rather than the wording.

At t=0.50 I am targeting a moderate mid-game utility around 0.65, improving slightly on the opponent’s latest offer while keeping most of their signaled structure. The opponent’s messages are repetitive “balanced/win-win” cheap talk, but their actual offers show real movement on issueA and some flexibility on issueB/issueC, while they seem to prefer issueD at valueF. I therefore keep issueD at valueF and accept issueB valueB, but ask for issueC valueB since they have offered it before and it is valuable to me.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.63, conceding modestly from my prior position while keeping issueA at my highest-value option because it is my most important issue. The opponent’s latest offer drops sharply in value for me, so I am not accepting that, but their t=0.50 offer signaled willingness to give issueA:valueA and issueB:valueB; I am using that as the basis for a plausible agreement and conceding issueC/issueD accordingly.
